emails.subject: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: Budget Spreadsheet Attached is the strawman. Note that the Sheet 2--drops participation to bare essentials. Sheet 3 assumes a 40% budget cut from Sheet 1. The deleted actions are different in Sheet 2 and Sheet 3. Jeff: Please review ASAP, add the gas matters to Sheet 1, delete gas matters that are not essential in Sheet 2, and take a 40% haircut in Sheet 3. Also add back any other matters that I have deleted that you disagree with, but make sure that Sheet 3 continues to meet the 40% haircut target. Then send to Sue Mara for review. Sue: Please review ASAP after Jeff. I need to get this into Rick by Wednesday. Alan: Please take a look and pass any comments along to me.

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: RE: Budget Spreadsheet What time on Wednesday? To: Dasovich, Jeff; Comnes, Alan; Mara, Susan

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: That's helpful. Thanks. Needed a time frame. Today's going to be very interesting. Should be able to get you something before 10; before noon, max. Best, Jeff

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: RE: Budget Spreadsheet Did anyone else get the attachment? If not, Paul, could you resend? Thanks. To: Dasovich, Jeff; Comnes, Alan; Mara, Susan

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: Thanks. Your attention is appreciated. I know your swamped and focused on other things. But these administrative details are important. It's what keeps the trains running on time the electricity flowing through the wires it's why you can go into a McDonald's from Poughkeepsie to Pitsburgh and the Big Macs taste the same . To: Kaufman, Paul; Comnes, Alan; Mara, Susan

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: For you, anything. My kingdom for a Kaufman... To: Dasovich, Jeff

emails.subject: RE: Budget Spreadsheet
emails.body: You ever have one of those days, where you feel like Hal (in the movie 2001)--while Dave was pulling out Hal's memory boards? To: Kaufman, Paul
